stories.headline: April crypto hacks hit $606M in 18 days — worst month since Bybit's Feb 2025 breach
stories.tldr: Crypto protocols have lost $606M to hacks in just the first 18 days of April, the worst monthly tally since Bybit's February 2025 breach.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Attackers have pivoted from smart contracts to private keys and signing infrastructure, and DefiLlama's data shows incidents now hitting roughly every 2.9 days in 2026 — insurance, custody, and audit assumptions all need a rewrite.
the_details: KelpDAO's rsETH bridge was drained for $292M on April 18; LayerZero and Kelp say evidence points to a state-backed actor, with North Korea's Lazarus Group as the lead suspect.
Drift Protocol on Solana lost $285M on April 1 via a long-running social engineering campaign that planted hidden pre-signed authorizations.
Together those two events account for roughly 95% of April's losses, with the remaining ~$30M spread across smaller DeFi exploits.

stories.headline: DeFi exodus accelerates: Aave bleeds $8.4B and TVL drops $13B post-KelpDAO
stories.tldr: More than $8.4B in deposits left Aave in the 48 hours after the KelpDAO and Drift hacks, dragging total DeFi TVL down by over $13B.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The flow shock is a confidence event, not a yield one — depositors are de-risking restaking and bridge exposure faster than protocols can re-onboard them, which compresses borrow rates and hits LST issuers' fee runways.
the_details: Aave's $8.4B outflow is the lender's largest 48-hour deposit drop on record outside of stablecoin depegs.
Liquid restaking tokens including rsETH and ezETH traded at sustained discounts to ETH as bridge confidence eroded.
Stablecoin reallocation to centralized venues spiked, with on-chain analysts flagging a parallel $5B USDT supply expansion as redeployment cushion.

stories.headline: Four central banks set rates this week as crypto braces for synchronized macro pivot
stories.tldr: The Fed, BoJ, ECB and BoE all deliver policy decisions during the week of April 27, the first synchronized rate week of 2026.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A coordinated dovish read would unlock the dollar bid that's been capping bitcoin at $80K, while any hawkish surprise from the BoJ would tighten the yen carry trade that has quietly funded much of this cycle's risk-on flow.
the_details: BTC and DXY are running in their most negatively-correlated stretch in nearly four years, per CoinDesk daybook data.
Robinhood and Galaxy Digital both report earnings the same week, giving the first read on retail crypto activity since Q1.
Bitcoin is on track for its best month in a year, helped by a $5B expansion in USDT supply over April.

stories.headline: Solana Alpenglow goes live with sub-200ms finality, but spot SOL ETF inflows crater 92%
stories.tldr: Solana's Alpenglow upgrade has cut transaction finality below 200ms in production, even as spot SOL ETF inflows collapsed 92% from November highs.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The tech is delivering on its roadmap, but the institutional demand thesis isn't keeping pace — issuers are now competing for a much smaller pool of dedicated SOL allocators than the November launch suggested.
the_details: Spot SOL ETF inflows fell to $34M in April from $419M in November 2025, a 92% drop in five months.
Anza and Jump Crypto's Firedancer team independently converged on Falcon-based post-quantum signatures for SOL.
Agave 4.1 client is targeting Q3 2026 testnet and Q4 2026 mainnet for the next finality push.

stories.headline: TOKEN2049 Dubai pushed to 2027 over heightened UAE security risks
stories.tldr: TOKEN2049's flagship Dubai event has been pushed from 2026 to April 21–22, 2027, with organizers citing heightened security risks across the UAE.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Dubai has been the de facto hub for sovereign-wealth and Gulf-family-office crypto capital — losing the on-the-ground convening window will reroute deal flow toward Singapore and Abu Dhabi events for the rest of the year.
the_details: Organizers confirmed the move on The Block, citing the broader regional security environment rather than a venue-specific issue.
TOKEN2049 Singapore remains scheduled, keeping at least one major APAC convening anchor on the 2026 calendar.
Sponsorship contracts are being rolled forward, per organizers, with no refund window opened.
